description: Classic folklore vulnerabilities: iron, salt, sunlight, running water, and ancient protections.
weaknesses: Salt Boundary
A thin line of salt is an impassable wall. It stops at the ocean's edge, avoids preserved foods, and flinches from the grain in a traveler's pocket.
Running Water Block
Streams and rivers form barriers it cannot cross. Its territory ends at the riverbank, and drought follows in its wake.
Iron Touch Weakness
Cold iron burns like acid on contact. No iron exists in its lair, its servants carry other metals, and old folk remedies all mention the same cure.
Sunlight Dissolution
Direct sunlight does not wound it. It unmakes it entirely. It keeps deep underground and fears the dawn like a living thing.
Grave Dirt Binding
Soil from its original burial plot anchors it like a chain. Old cemetery records hint at the site, and it avoids one particular graveyard without explanation.
Silver Thread Cutting
Silver thread slices through its defenses like wire through clay. It avoids seamstresses, and no silver embroidery exists anywhere in its domain.
Milk Vulnerability
Fresh milk burns it like acid. It avoids farms, keeps no dairy, and its mere presence curdles milk in the pail.
Crossroads Confusion
At any crossroads it loses all sense of direction, forced to wander aimlessly. It takes circuitous routes and never builds its lair near an intersection.
Horseshoe Iron
A horseshoe over the door is more than superstition. Its touch burns worse than common iron, and rural blacksmiths have always known why.
Mustard Seed Burn
Scattered mustard seeds form a barrier it cannot cross. It avoids certain cuisines, and spice merchants in the region sleep soundly.
Unbroken Circle
A perfectly drawn circle is an impenetrable cage. It breaks circular patterns wherever it finds them and builds only in sharp angles.
Threshold Permission
It cannot enter a home without invitation from someone who lives there. It manipulates, flatters, and begs at the doorstep, but it cannot step inside uninvited.
Bread and Salt Peace
Ancient hospitality laws bind it. Those who have shared bread and salt with it are untouchable, and it avoids dinner invitations at all costs.
Homemade Protection
Items crafted by loving hands ward it off. Mass production means nothing, but a grandmother's knitting is armor. It fears hearths where things are made by hand.
Autumn Leaf Shield
Fallen autumn leaves grant complete protection. It destroys deciduous trees when it can and retreats when the leaves begin to turn.
Ancient Tree Weakness
Wood from ancient trees burns it on contact. It avoids old-growth forests, and druids have always known which groves to protect.
